brief , the practice of the christian-sabbath stand chief in two thing , viz. in preparation to it , and sanctification of it . 1. there must be solemn and serious preparation the evening before , to get our heart into a sabbath frame , this the lord call for in the very front of the commandment , remember the sabbath-day to keep it holy , remember it before it come , that you may keep it holy when it come ; this word [ remember ] shall be as a bell to toll we in from our shop and farm , to our chamber and closet , there to commune with our own heart , to cast up our spiritual account , to call over our want , and ( if we have heart ) to weep over our week day sin , and stir up our self to take hold of Christ , that we may make peace with God , they that have any acquaintance with heartwork , find it hard to have to do with a dusty world , full of sin and snare , and not be defile or entangle with it ; earthly thing be apt to leave a tincture upon the most holy and heavenly heart : there must therefore be a rub off this rust of the world , a wash these dirty heart and hand before we be fit to draw nigh to God in solemn worship ; CHAP. xxxii . contain a use of exhortation . it be Solomons counsel , Pro. 27. 23. be thou diligent to know the state of thy flock , and look well ( in the hebrew , set thy heart unto it ) to thy herd ; and i may say as the apostle , 1 Cor. 9 9 , 10. do god take care for ox , and sheep , or do he speak this altogether for our sake ? may i not say rather , be diligent to know the state of thy , heart , and of that flock of thought , which be a great business to look well too , then to keep a flock of sheep , or herd of swine : a shame it be for man to look after every thing and not himself , to know every city , country , art , language , science , and not to know himself ; to observe man , time custom every thing , and not to observe himself , and the custom and manner of his own heart and thought . the grammarian make his latin by rule , the poet make his verse by rule , the workman build his house by rule , and must we have no rule for our heart and thought ? the heaven and every star move by rule , day and night come and go by rule , summer , winter , seedtime and harvest come and go by rule , yea the sea ebb and flow by rule , and the bird of the air , the swallow and cuckoo , come and go by rule , and shall man be the only unruly creature , and have Jer. 8. 7. no rule for his heart and thought to move by ? and be as the cloud of the air , or wave of the sea , or a ship without compass or pilot , that be drive up and down without guide or stay ? be it not matter of shame to see we shall keep house clean , face clean , hand clean , and all our clothes must be clean , or we be ashamed of our self , and the heart unclean . garden must be weed , house must be wash , household stuff must be scour , door must be sweep , swine and poultry must be rend , and the heart neglect ; strange it be we shall observe order in every thing and affect neatness , every thing must have his fit place , the horse have his stable , the ox his crib , the dog his kennel , the swine their coat , that horse , dog , and swine , eat not , nor lie together ; that we shall have no partition in our heart , good and evil thought tumble together . we keep door to our house , yea to every room , lock , and bolt , or latch to every door , yea we have door and iron bar to our very stable , and none to the heart . as a city without watch and ward , gate and bar , or house without Pro. 25. 28. door and lock , so be the man , say Solomon , that have no rule over his own spirit ; we have hedge and ditch about our ground , fold to keep in our sheep , and to keep out other cattle , and shall our heart only lie open and unfenced , as commons and waste without any regard ; we put our very swine to a keeper , and we have a horsekeeper , and shall we have no heart-keeper ; Xiv . but of poor there be two sort ; god poor , and the devil : impotent poor , and impudent poor : the poor upon who we shall exercise our beneficence , be the honest labourer , and the poor householder ;
